words.focus: 重点掌握“车辆”，并能在阅读中识别 motor vehicle。

example.en: Even though “motor vehicle” occurs frequently in formal texts, its force becomes fully apparent only when the reader identifies the clause that it modifies and the assumption that the writer expects the audience to accept.
example.zh: 尽管“motor vehicle（汽车；机动车辆）”常见于正式文本，但只有当读者找出它所修饰的从句以及作者希望受众接受的假设时，它的作用才会完全显现。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 vehicle 和“motor vehicle（汽车；机动车辆）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“发行”，并能在阅读中识别 key issue。

example.en: The editor, who retained the expression “key issue” after several revisions, explained that it conveyed a distinction which a simpler word would have failed to preserve.
example.zh: 编辑经过多次修改后仍保留了“key issue（关键议题）”这一表达，因为他认为它传达了一种更简单的词无法保留的区别。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 issue 和“key issue（关键议题）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“国内的”，并能在阅读中识别 domestic market。

example.en: Even though “domestic market” occurs frequently in formal texts, its force becomes fully apparent only when the reader identifies the clause that it modifies and the assumption that the writer expects the audience to accept.

words.memory: 抓住 -ic（形容词后缀）；再和“domestic market”连成词块记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“另外；要不然”，并能在阅读中识别 or otherwise。

example.en: When “or otherwise” appears in a report whose author is comparing competing explanations, readers should ask whether the expression introduces evidence or merely qualifies a claim that has already been made.
example.zh: 当“or otherwise（或相反；或其反面）”出现在一篇比较不同解释的报告中时，读者应判断该表达是在引入证据，还是仅仅限定前文已经提出的观点。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 otherwise 和“or otherwise（或相反；或其反面）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“路，路线，路程”，并能在阅读中识别 en route。

example.en: The editor, who retained the expression “en route” after several revisions, explained that it conveyed a distinction which a simpler word would have failed to preserve.
example.zh: 编辑经过多次修改后仍保留了“en route（（法）在途中）”这一表达，因为他认为它传达了一种更简单的词无法保留的区别。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 route 和“en route（（法）在途中）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“出现；合乎情理”，并能在阅读中识别 public figure。

example.en: Although the expression “public figure” may seem familiar, students often misinterpret it when the surrounding paragraph contrasts an immediate result with a consequence that will emerge only later.
example.zh: 尽管“public figure（社会名人）”这一表达看似熟悉，但当上下文把即时结果与之后才会显现的后果作对比时，学生往往会误解它。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 figure 和“public figure（社会名人）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“重要的”，并能在阅读中识别 significant difference。

example.en: Because “significant difference” can carry a more precise meaning in academic writing than it does in casual conversation, anyone who translates the sentence mechanically may overlook the argument on which the conclusion depends.
example.zh: 由于“significant difference（显著性差异）”在学术写作中的含义可能比在日常对话中更精确，机械翻译句子的人可能会忽略结论赖以成立的论证。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 significant 和“significant difference（显著性差异）”一起记。

example.zh: “core competence”之所以值得掌握，是因为它不仅表达“核心竞争力；核心才能”，还帮助作者把观察结果与一个否则可能显得缺乏依据的更广泛结论联系起来。

以……方式处理；向……提出建议

example.en: Although the expression “new approach” may seem familiar, students often misinterpret it when the surrounding paragraph contrasts an immediate result with a consequence that will emerge only later.
example.zh: 尽管“new approach（新方案；新做法）”这一表达看似熟悉，但当上下文把即时结果与之后才会显现的后果作对比时，学生往往会误解它。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 approach 和“new approach（新方案；新做法）”一起记。

words.memory: 抓住 -al（形容词后缀）；再和“external environment”连成词块记。

example.en: The editor, who retained the expression “obtain employment” after several revisions, explained that it conveyed a distinction which a simpler word would have failed to preserve.
example.zh: 编辑经过多次修改后仍保留了“obtain employment（就业，找到工作）”这一表达，因为他认为它传达了一种更简单的词无法保留的区别。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 obtain 和“obtain employment（就业，找到工作）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“客观的；无偏见的”，并能在阅读中识别 objective function。

words.memory: 抓住 -ive（形容词后缀）；再和“objective function”连成词块记。

example.en: Even though “pursue” occurs frequently in formal texts, its force becomes fully apparent only when the reader identifies the clause that it modifies and the assumption that the writer expects the audience to accept.

example.en: What makes “frequently asked questions” worth learning is that the expression not only conveys “常见问答（等于FAQ）” but also helps the writer connect an observation with a broader conclusion that may otherwise seem unsupported.
example.zh: “frequently asked questions”之所以值得掌握，是因为它不仅表达“常见问答（等于FAQ）”，还帮助作者把观察结果与一个否则可能显得缺乏依据的更广泛结论联系起来。

words.memory: 抓住 -ly（副词后缀）；再和“frequently asked questions”连成词块记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“视；想象力”，并能在阅读中识别 computer vision。

example.en: Even though “computer vision” occurs frequently in formal texts, its force becomes fully apparent only when the reader identifies the clause that it modifies and the assumption that the writer expects the audience to accept.
example.zh: 尽管“computer vision（计算机视觉；机械视觉）”常见于正式文本，但只有当读者找出它所修饰的从句以及作者希望受众接受的假设时，它的作用才会完全显现。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 vision 和“computer vision（计算机视觉；机械视觉）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“保留,留用”，并能在阅读中识别 retain。

example.en: The editor, who retained the expression “retain” after several revisions, explained that it conveyed a distinction which a simpler word would have failed to preserve.
example.zh: 编辑经过多次修改后仍保留了“retain（保留,留用）”这一表达，因为他认为它传达了一种更简单的词无法保留的区别。

words.memory: 边拼边分音节读 retain，再用核心义“retain”回想语境。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 rigid 和“rigid body（刚体；刚性体）”一起记。

example.en: The editor, who retained the expression “deputy director” after several revisions, explained that it conveyed a distinction which a simpler word would have failed to preserve.
example.zh: 编辑经过多次修改后仍保留了“deputy director（副局长，副厅长；副院长）”这一表达，因为他认为它传达了一种更简单的词无法保留的区别。

example.en: Although the expression “on loan” may seem familiar, students often misinterpret it when the surrounding paragraph contrasts an immediate result with a consequence that will emerge only later.
example.zh: 尽管“on loan（借用；借贷）”这一表达看似熟悉，但当上下文把即时结果与之后才会显现的后果作对比时，学生往往会误解它。

words.focus: 重点掌握“相隔；分开”，并能在阅读中识别 apart from。

example.en: When “apart from” appears in a report whose author is comparing competing explanations, readers should ask whether the expression introduces evidence or merely qualifies a claim that has already been made.
example.zh: 当“apart from（远离，除……之外；且不说）”出现在一篇比较不同解释的报告中时，读者应判断该表达是在引入证据，还是仅仅限定前文已经提出的观点。

example.zh: 尽管“in proportion（成比例；相称）”这一表达看似熟悉，但当上下文把即时结果与之后才会显现的后果作对比时，学生往往会误解它。

words.memory: 抓住 -tion（名词后缀）；再和“in proportion”连成词块记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“显示；陈列，展览”，并能在阅读中识别 on exhibit。

example.en: What makes “on exhibit” worth learning is that the expression not only conveys “展出” but also helps the writer connect an observation with a broader conclusion that may otherwise seem unsupported.
example.zh: “on exhibit”之所以值得掌握，是因为它不仅表达“展出”，还帮助作者把观察结果与一个否则可能显得缺乏依据的更广泛结论联系起来。

words.memory: 按 ex + hibit 拆写；ex-常提示“向外、以前”。

words.focus: 重点掌握“有效的；有影响的”，并能在阅读中识别 effective management。

example.en: Because “effective management” can carry a more precise meaning in academic writing than it does in casual conversation, anyone who translates the sentence mechanically may overlook the argument on which the conclusion depends.
example.zh: 由于“effective management（有效管理）”在学术写作中的含义可能比在日常对话中更精确，机械翻译句子的人可能会忽略结论赖以成立的论证。

words.memory: 抓住 -ive（形容词后缀）；再和“effective management”连成词块记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“鬼，灵魂；鬼魂”，并能在阅读中识别 ghost of a。

collocations.zh: 丝毫，微量，一点儿(常在否定句中与 chance，idea 连用，或与

fixedPhrases.zh: 丝毫，微量，一点儿(常在否定句中与 chance，idea 连用，或与

example.en: Because “ghost of a” can carry a more precise meaning in academic writing than it does in casual conversation, anyone who translates the sentence mechanically may overlook the argument on which the conclusion depends.
example.zh: 由于“ghost of a（丝毫，微量，一点儿(常在否定句中与 chance，idea 连用，或与 ）”在学术写作中的含义可能比在日常对话中更精确，机械翻译句子的人可能会忽略结论赖以成立的论证。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 ghost 和“ghost of a（丝毫，微量，一点儿(常在否定句中与 chance，idea 连用，或与 ）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“装配”，并能在阅读中识别 assembly line。

example.en: Although the expression “assembly line” may seem familiar, students often misinterpret it when the surrounding paragraph contrasts an immediate result with a consequence that will emerge only later.
example.zh: 尽管“assembly line（装配线；流水作业线）”这一表达看似熟悉，但当上下文把即时结果与之后才会显现的后果作对比时，学生往往会误解它。

words.memory: 抓住 -ly（副词后缀）；再和“assembly line”连成词块记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“调查，审视，仔细考虑”，并能在阅读中识别 questionnaire survey。

example.en: When “questionnaire survey” appears in a report whose author is comparing competing explanations, readers should ask whether the expression introduces evidence or merely qualifies a claim that has already been made.
example.zh: 当“questionnaire survey（问卷调查）”出现在一篇比较不同解释的报告中时，读者应判断该表达是在引入证据，还是仅仅限定前文已经提出的观点。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 survey 和“questionnaire survey（问卷调查）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“为数众多的；许多”，并能在阅读中识别 numerous。

example.en: Although the expression “numerous” may seem familiar, students often misinterpret it when the surrounding paragraph contrasts an immediate result with a consequence that will emerge only later.
example.zh: 尽管“numerous（为数众多的）”这一表达看似熟悉，但当上下文把即时结果与之后才会显现的后果作对比时，学生往往会误解它。

words.memory: 抓住 -ous（形容词后缀）帮助判断词性。

words.focus: 重点掌握“直接地；立即”，并能在阅读中识别 directly proportional。

example.en: Although the expression “directly proportional” may seem familiar, students often misinterpret it when the surrounding paragraph contrasts an immediate result with a consequence that will emerge only later.
example.zh: 尽管“directly proportional（直接比例的）”这一表达看似熟悉，但当上下文把即时结果与之后才会显现的后果作对比时，学生往往会误解它。

words.focus: 重点掌握“魔鬼，恶魔”，并能在阅读中识别 like the devil。

collocations.zh: 讨厌……的，异常的……，非常恼人(或糟糕)的；有趣的

fixedPhrases.zh: 讨厌……的，异常的……，非常恼人(或糟糕)的；有趣的

example.en: Even though “like the devil” occurs frequently in formal texts, its force becomes fully apparent only when the reader identifies the clause that it modifies and the assumption that the writer expects the audience to accept.
example.zh: 尽管“like the devil（猛烈地）”常见于正式文本，但只有当读者找出它所修饰的从句以及作者希望受众接受的假设时，它的作用才会完全显现。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 devil 和“like the devil（猛烈地）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“使合适”，并能在阅读中识别 bevel gear。

collocations.zh: 锥齿轮，锥形齿轮；伞齿轮

fixedPhrases.zh: 处于正常状态；处于就绪状态

example.en: The editor, who retained the expression “bevel gear” after several revisions, explained that it conveyed a distinction which a simpler word would have failed to preserve.
example.zh: 编辑经过多次修改后仍保留了“bevel gear（锥齿轮，锥形齿轮；伞齿轮）”这一表达，因为他认为它传达了一种更简单的词无法保留的区别。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 gear 和“bevel gear（锥齿轮，锥形齿轮；伞齿轮）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“诗，诗歌，诗作”，并能在阅读中识别 poetry creation。

example.en: Because “poetry creation” can carry a more precise meaning in academic writing than it does in casual conversation, anyone who translates the sentence mechanically may overlook the argument on which the conclusion depends.
example.zh: 由于“poetry creation（诗歌文本）”在学术写作中的含义可能比在日常对话中更精确，机械翻译句子的人可能会忽略结论赖以成立的论证。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 poetry 和“poetry creation（诗歌文本）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“地基；基础”，并能在阅读中识别 on the foundation。

collocations.zh: 领取基金会提供奖学金(或津贴)的；属于由基金维持的机构的

fixedPhrases.zh: 领取基金会提供奖学金(或津贴)的；属于由基金维持的机构的

example.en: What makes “on the foundation” worth learning is that the expression not only conveys “领取基金会提供奖学金(或津贴)的；属于由基金维持的机构的” but also helps the writer connect an observation with a broader conclusion that may otherwise seem unsupported.
example.zh: “on the foundation”之所以值得掌握，是因为它不仅表达“领取基金会提供奖学金(或津贴)的；属于由基金维持的机构的”，还帮助作者把观察结果与一个否则可能显得缺乏依据的更广泛结论联系起来。

words.memory: 抓住 -tion（名词后缀）；再和“on the foundation”连成词块记。

example.en: Although the expression “completely” may seem familiar, students often misinterpret it when the surrounding paragraph contrasts an immediate result with a consequence that will emerge only later.
example.zh: 尽管“completely（十分，完全地）”这一表达看似熟悉，但当上下文把即时结果与之后才会显现的后果作对比时，学生往往会误解它。

words.memory: 抓住 -ly（副词后缀）帮助判断词性。

words.focus: 重点掌握“迷，神秘的事物”，并能在阅读中识别 murder mystery。

example.en: What makes “murder mystery” worth learning is that the expression not only conveys “谋杀之谜” but also helps the writer connect an observation with a broader conclusion that may otherwise seem unsupported.
example.zh: “murder mystery”之所以值得掌握，是因为它不仅表达“谋杀之谜”，还帮助作者把观察结果与一个否则可能显得缺乏依据的更广泛结论联系起来。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 mystery 和“murder mystery（谋杀之谜）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“咨询”，并能在阅读中识别 consult with。

example.en: When “consult with” appears in a report whose author is comparing competing explanations, readers should ask whether the expression introduces evidence or merely qualifies a claim that has already been made.
example.zh: 当“consult with（商量，协商）”出现在一篇比较不同解释的报告中时，读者应判断该表达是在引入证据，还是仅仅限定前文已经提出的观点。

words.memory: 按 co + nsult 拆写；co-常提示“共同”。

words.focus: 重点掌握“排除，不包括”，并能在阅读中识别 exclude。

example.en: When “exclude” appears in a report whose author is comparing competing explanations, readers should ask whether the expression introduces evidence or merely qualifies a claim that has already been made.
example.zh: 当“exclude（排除，不包括）”出现在一篇比较不同解释的报告中时，读者应判断该表达是在引入证据，还是仅仅限定前文已经提出的观点。

example.en: What makes “offensive player” worth learning is that the expression not only conveys “进攻球员；进攻队员” but also helps the writer connect an observation with a broader conclusion that may otherwise seem unsupported.
example.zh: “offensive player”之所以值得掌握，是因为它不仅表达“进攻球员；进攻队员”，还帮助作者把观察结果与一个否则可能显得缺乏依据的更广泛结论联系起来。

example.en: Even though “give priority to” occurs frequently in formal texts, its force becomes fully apparent only when the reader identifies the clause that it modifies and the assumption that the writer expects the audience to accept.
example.zh: 尽管“give priority to（优先考虑；认为优先）”常见于正式文本，但只有当读者找出它所修饰的从句以及作者希望受众接受的假设时，它的作用才会完全显现。

example.zh: 当“chill out（冷静，冷静下来）”出现在一篇比较不同解释的报告中时，读者应判断该表达是在引入证据，还是仅仅限定前文已经提出的观点。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 chill 和“chill out（冷静，冷静下来）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“巨大的”，并能在阅读中识别 enormous。

example.en: Although the expression “enormous” may seem familiar, students often misinterpret it when the surrounding paragraph contrasts an immediate result with a consequence that will emerge only later.
example.zh: 尽管“enormous（巨大的）”这一表达看似熟悉，但当上下文把即时结果与之后才会显现的后果作对比时，学生往往会误解它。

words.memory: 抓住 -ous（形容词后缀）帮助判断词性。

example.en: Even though “no offense” occurs frequently in formal texts, its force becomes fully apparent only when the reader identifies the clause that it modifies and the assumption that the writer expects the audience to accept.
example.zh: 尽管“no offense（无意冒犯；请勿见怪）”常见于正式文本，但只有当读者找出它所修饰的从句以及作者希望受众接受的假设时，它的作用才会完全显现。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 offense 和“no offense（无意冒犯；请勿见怪）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“以……打赌， 拿……冒险”，并能在阅读中识别 at stake。

collocations.zh: 为某人取得某物而提供金钱(或援助)，资助某人取得某物

fixedPhrases.zh: 为某人取得某物而提供金钱(或援助)，资助某人取得某物

example.en: When “at stake” appears in a report whose author is comparing competing explanations, readers should ask whether the expression introduces evidence or merely qualifies a claim that has already been made.
example.zh: 当“at stake（危如累卵；处于危险中）”出现在一篇比较不同解释的报告中时，读者应判断该表达是在引入证据，还是仅仅限定前文已经提出的观点。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 stake 和“at stake（危如累卵；处于危险中）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“忧虑的；渴望的”，并能在阅读中识别 anxious about。

example.en: The editor, who retained the expression “anxious about” after several revisions, explained that it conveyed a distinction which a simpler word would have failed to preserve.
example.zh: 编辑经过多次修改后仍保留了“anxious about（为…………担心；对…………着急）”这一表达，因为他认为它传达了一种更简单的词无法保留的区别。

words.memory: 抓住 -ous（形容词后缀）；再和“anxious about”连成词块记。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 colony 和“colony formation（群体形成；集落形成）”一起记。

example.zh: 当“anticipate（预感）”出现在一篇比较不同解释的报告中时，读者应判断该表达是在引入证据，还是仅仅限定前文已经提出的观点。

words.memory: 抓住 -ate（常见动词后缀）帮助判断词性。

words.focus: 重点掌握“迷人的，可爱的”，并能在阅读中识别 prince charming。

collocations.zh: 对女子假装殷勤的男子；女子理想中的求婚者

example.en: The editor, who retained the expression “prince charming” after several revisions, explained that it conveyed a distinction which a simpler word would have failed to preserve.
example.zh: 编辑经过多次修改后仍保留了“prince charming（对女子假装殷勤的男子；女子理想中的求婚者）”这一表达，因为他认为它传达了一种更简单的词无法保留的区别。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 charming 和“prince charming（对女子假装殷勤的男子；女子理想中的求婚者）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“发白热光”，并能在阅读中识别 glow discharge。

example.en: Because “glow discharge” can carry a more precise meaning in academic writing than it does in casual conversation, anyone who translates the sentence mechanically may overlook the argument on which the conclusion depends.
example.zh: 由于“glow discharge（辉光放电）”在学术写作中的含义可能比在日常对话中更精确，机械翻译句子的人可能会忽略结论赖以成立的论证。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 glow 和“glow discharge（辉光放电）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“匿名的；无名的”，并能在阅读中识别 alcoholics anonymous。

collocations.zh: 匿名戒酒互助社；慝名戒毒会

example.en: What makes “alcoholics anonymous” worth learning is that the expression not only conveys “匿名戒酒互助社；慝名戒毒会” but also helps the writer connect an observation with a broader conclusion that may otherwise seem unsupported.
example.zh: “alcoholics anonymous”之所以值得掌握，是因为它不仅表达“匿名戒酒互助社；慝名戒毒会”，还帮助作者把观察结果与一个否则可能显得缺乏依据的更广泛结论联系起来。

words.memory: 抓住 -ous（形容词后缀）；再和“alcoholics anonymous”连成词块记。

words.memory: 抓住 -ify（动词后缀）；再和“qualify for”连成词块记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“抱怨”，并能在阅读中识别 complain about。

example.zh: 尽管“replacement cost（重置成本）”常见于正式文本，但只有当读者找出它所修饰的从句以及作者希望受众接受的假设时，它的作用才会完全显现。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 spill 和“oil spill（漏油；浮油）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“学期”，并能在阅读中识别 fall semester。

example.en: When “fall semester” appears in a report whose author is comparing competing explanations, readers should ask whether the expression introduces evidence or merely qualifies a claim that has already been made.
example.zh: 当“fall semester（秋季班，秋季学期）”出现在一篇比较不同解释的报告中时，读者应判断该表达是在引入证据，还是仅仅限定前文已经提出的观点。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 semester 和“fall semester（秋季班，秋季学期）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“把……编结”，并能在阅读中识别 knit fabric。

example.en: Although the expression “knit fabric” may seem familiar, students often misinterpret it when the surrounding paragraph contrasts an immediate result with a consequence that will emerge only later.
example.zh: 尽管“knit fabric（织物，针织布）”这一表达看似熟悉，但当上下文把即时结果与之后才会显现的后果作对比时，学生往往会误解它。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 knit 和“knit fabric（织物，针织布）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“管理的”，并能在阅读中识别 administrative region。

example.en: When “administrative region” appears in a report whose author is comparing competing explanations, readers should ask whether the expression introduces evidence or merely qualifies a claim that has already been made.
example.zh: 当“administrative region（行政区域；行政分区）”出现在一篇比较不同解释的报告中时，读者应判断该表达是在引入证据，还是仅仅限定前文已经提出的观点。

words.memory: 抓住 -ive（形容词后缀）；再和“administrative region”连成词块记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“在室内， 在户内”，并能在阅读中识别 indoor air pollution。

example.en: Because “indoor air pollution” can carry a more precise meaning in academic writing than it does in casual conversation, anyone who translates the sentence mechanically may overlook the argument on which the conclusion depends.
example.zh: 由于“indoor air pollution（空内空气污染）”在学术写作中的含义可能比在日常对话中更精确，机械翻译句子的人可能会忽略结论赖以成立的论证。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 indoor 和“indoor air pollution（空内空气污染）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“供应；预备”，并能在阅读中识别 make provision for。

collocations.zh: 为……预先采取措施；为……作好准备

fixedPhrases.zh: 为……预先采取措施；为……作好准备

example.en: What makes “make provision for” worth learning is that the expression not only conveys “为……预先采取措施；为……作好准备” but also helps the writer connect an observation with a broader conclusion that may otherwise seem unsupported.
example.zh: “make provision for”之所以值得掌握，是因为它不仅表达“为……预先采取措施；为……作好准备”，还帮助作者把观察结果与一个否则可能显得缺乏依据的更广泛结论联系起来。

words.memory: 抓住 -sion（名词后缀）；再和“make provision for”连成词块记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“飘然而行”，并能在阅读中识别 spring breeze。

fixedPhrases.zh: 轻而易举地取胜；象一阵风似地来到

example.en: The editor, who retained the expression “spring breeze” after several revisions, explained that it conveyed a distinction which a simpler word would have failed to preserve.
example.zh: 编辑经过多次修改后仍保留了“spring breeze（春风；望春风（台湾名歌））”这一表达，因为他认为它传达了一种更简单的词无法保留的区别。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 breeze 和“spring breeze（春风；望春风（台湾名歌））”一起记。

example.en: Even though “necessarily” occurs frequently in formal texts, its force becomes fully apparent only when the reader identifies the clause that it modifies and the assumption that the writer expects the audience to accept.
example.zh: 尽管“necessarily（必然，必定）”常见于正式文本，但只有当读者找出它所修饰的从句以及作者希望受众接受的假设时，它的作用才会完全显现。

words.memory: 抓住 -ly（副词后缀）帮助判断词性。

collocations.zh: 美感，审美观念；审美知觉

example.en: The editor, who retained the expression “visual perception” after several revisions, explained that it conveyed a distinction which a simpler word would have failed to preserve.
example.zh: 编辑经过多次修改后仍保留了“visual perception（视觉感知；视知觉）”这一表达，因为他认为它传达了一种更简单的词无法保留的区别。

words.memory: 抓住 -tion（名词后缀）；再和“visual perception”连成词块记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“最远的”，并能在阅读中识别 to the utmost。

example.en: Although the expression “to the utmost” may seem familiar, students often misinterpret it when the surrounding paragraph contrasts an immediate result with a consequence that will emerge only later.
example.zh: 尽管“to the utmost（极度；尽力）”这一表达看似熟悉，但当上下文把即时结果与之后才会显现的后果作对比时，学生往往会误解它。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 utmost 和“to the utmost（极度；尽力）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“接受，验收；承认”，并能在阅读中识别 final acceptance。

example.en: What makes “final acceptance” worth learning is that the expression not only conveys “验收；最终验收” but also helps the writer connect an observation with a broader conclusion that may otherwise seem unsupported.
example.zh: “final acceptance”之所以值得掌握，是因为它不仅表达“验收；最终验收”，还帮助作者把观察结果与一个否则可能显得缺乏依据的更广泛结论联系起来。

words.memory: 抓住 -ance（名词后缀）；再和“final acceptance”连成词块记。

example.zh: 由于“initiate（开始，发起）”在学术写作中的含义可能比在日常对话中更精确，机械翻译句子的人可能会忽略结论赖以成立的论证。

words.memory: 抓住 -ate（常见动词后缀）帮助判断词性。

words.focus: 重点掌握“特有的；特别的”，并能在阅读中识别 peculiar。

example.en: The editor, who retained the expression “peculiar” after several revisions, explained that it conveyed a distinction which a simpler word would have failed to preserve.
example.zh: 编辑经过多次修改后仍保留了“peculiar（特有的）”这一表达，因为他认为它传达了一种更简单的词无法保留的区别。

words.memory: 边拼边分音节读 peculiar，再用核心义“peculiar”回想语境。

words.focus: 重点掌握“俘虏，被监禁的人”，并能在阅读中识别 captive audience。

collocations.zh: 受制而走不开的听众或观众

example.en: Even though “captive audience” occurs frequently in formal texts, its force becomes fully apparent only when the reader identifies the clause that it modifies and the assumption that the writer expects the audience to accept.
example.zh: 尽管“captive audience（受制而走不开的听众或观众）”常见于正式文本，但只有当读者找出它所修饰的从句以及作者希望受众接受的假设时，它的作用才会完全显现。

words.memory: 抓住 -ive（形容词后缀）；再和“captive audience”连成词块记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“履行；满足”，并能在阅读中识别 fulfil。

example.en: Although the expression “fulfil” may seem familiar, students often misinterpret it when the surrounding paragraph contrasts an immediate result with a consequence that will emerge only later.
example.zh: 尽管“fulfil（履行）”这一表达看似熟悉，但当上下文把即时结果与之后才会显现的后果作对比时，学生往往会误解它。

words.memory: 边拼边分音节读 fulfil，再用核心义“fulfil”回想语境。

words.focus: 重点掌握“细长的；微薄的”，并能在阅读中识别 slender body。

example.en: What makes “slender body” worth learning is that the expression not only conveys “细长体；瘦长体” but also helps the writer connect an observation with a broader conclusion that may otherwise seem unsupported.
example.zh: “slender body”之所以值得掌握，是因为它不仅表达“细长体；瘦长体”，还帮助作者把观察结果与一个否则可能显得缺乏依据的更广泛结论联系起来。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 slender 和“slender body（细长体；瘦长体）”一起记。

example.zh: 尽管“thoughtful（沉思的）”这一表达看似熟悉，但当上下文把即时结果与之后才会显现的后果作对比时，学生往往会误解它。

words.memory: 抓住 -ful（形容词后缀）帮助判断词性。

confusables.tip: 用核心义和搭配建立两张不同的记忆卡。
用核心义和搭配建立两张不同的记忆卡。

words.focus: 重点掌握“炉子，熔炉；鼓风炉”，并能在阅读中识别 heating furnace。

example.en: When “heating furnace” appears in a report whose author is comparing competing explanations, readers should ask whether the expression introduces evidence or merely qualifies a claim that has already been made.
example.zh: 当“heating furnace（加热炉；热气炉）”出现在一篇比较不同解释的报告中时，读者应判断该表达是在引入证据，还是仅仅限定前文已经提出的观点。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 furnace 和“heating furnace（加热炉；热气炉）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“滑动；消逝”，并能在阅读中识别 glide。

example.en: The editor, who retained the expression “glide” after several revisions, explained that it conveyed a distinction which a simpler word would have failed to preserve.
example.zh: 编辑经过多次修改后仍保留了“glide（滑动）”这一表达，因为他认为它传达了一种更简单的词无法保留的区别。

words.memory: 边拼边分音节读 glide，再用核心义“glide”回想语境。

words.focus: 重点掌握“十分喜悦的，快乐的”，并能在阅读中识别 joyful。

example.en: What makes “joyful” worth learning is that the expression not only conveys “十分喜悦的，快乐的” but also helps the writer connect an observation with a broader conclusion that may otherwise seem unsupported.
example.zh: “joyful”之所以值得掌握，是因为它不仅表达“十分喜悦的，快乐的”，还帮助作者把观察结果与一个否则可能显得缺乏依据的更广泛结论联系起来。

words.memory: 边拼边分音节读 joyful，再用核心义“joyful”回想语境。

example.en: Although the expression “sociology of law” may seem familiar, students often misinterpret it when the surrounding paragraph contrasts an immediate result with a consequence that will emerge only later.
example.zh: 尽管“sociology of law（法律社会学）”这一表达看似熟悉，但当上下文把即时结果与之后才会显现的后果作对比时，学生往往会误解它。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 sociology 和“sociology of law（法律社会学）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“鄙视，蔑视”，并能在阅读中识别 despise。

example.en: What makes “despise” worth learning is that the expression not only conveys “鄙视，蔑视” but also helps the writer connect an observation with a broader conclusion that may otherwise seem unsupported.
example.zh: “despise”之所以值得掌握，是因为它不仅表达“鄙视，蔑视”，还帮助作者把观察结果与一个否则可能显得缺乏依据的更广泛结论联系起来。

words.memory: 抓住 -ise（动词后缀）帮助判断词性。

words.focus: 重点掌握“高兴的”，并能在阅读中识别 delightful。

example.en: When “delightful” appears in a report whose author is comparing competing explanations, readers should ask whether the expression introduces evidence or merely qualifies a claim that has already been made.

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 flourish 和“a flourish of trumpets（大肆宣扬）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“限制；限度，局限”，并能在阅读中识别 time limitation。

example.en: When “time limitation” appears in a report whose author is comparing competing explanations, readers should ask whether the expression introduces evidence or merely qualifies a claim that has already been made.
example.zh: 当“time limitation（时间限制）”出现在一篇比较不同解释的报告中时，读者应判断该表达是在引入证据，还是仅仅限定前文已经提出的观点。

words.memory: 抓住 -tion（名词后缀）；再和“time limitation”连成词块记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“强迫”，并能在阅读中识别 compel。

example.en: Even though “compel” occurs frequently in formal texts, its force becomes fully apparent only when the reader identifies the clause that it modifies and the assumption that the writer expects the audience to accept.
example.zh: 尽管“compel（强迫）”常见于正式文本，但只有当读者找出它所修饰的从句以及作者希望受众接受的假设时，它的作用才会完全显现。

words.memory: 边拼边分音节读 compel，再用核心义“compel”回想语境。

example.zh: 由于“shiver（颤抖，哆嗦）”在学术写作中的含义可能比在日常对话中更精确，机械翻译句子的人可能会忽略结论赖以成立的论证。

words.memory: 边拼边分音节读 shiver，再用核心义“shiver”回想语境。

words.focus: 重点掌握“真诚的，诚恳的”，并能在阅读中识别 cordial。

example.en: When “cordial” appears in a report whose author is comparing competing explanations, readers should ask whether the expression introduces evidence or merely qualifies a claim that has already been made.
example.zh: 当“cordial（真诚的，诚恳的）”出现在一篇比较不同解释的报告中时，读者应判断该表达是在引入证据，还是仅仅限定前文已经提出的观点。

words.memory: 抓住 -al（形容词后缀）帮助判断词性。

合乎情理地，符合逻辑地

words.focus: 重点掌握“代名词”，并能在阅读中识别 interrogative pronoun。

example.en: What makes “interrogative pronoun” worth learning is that the expression not only conveys “疑问代词” but also helps the writer connect an observation with a broader conclusion that may otherwise seem unsupported.
example.zh: “interrogative pronoun”之所以值得掌握，是因为它不仅表达“疑问代词”，还帮助作者把观察结果与一个否则可能显得缺乏依据的更广泛结论联系起来。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 pronoun 和“interrogative pronoun（疑问代词）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“引起，唤起；唤醒”，并能在阅读中识别 arouse the enthusiasm of。

example.en: The editor, who retained the expression “arouse the enthusiasm of” after several revisions, explained that it conveyed a distinction which a simpler word would have failed to preserve.
example.zh: 编辑经过多次修改后仍保留了“arouse the enthusiasm of（调动积极性）”这一表达，因为他认为它传达了一种更简单的词无法保留的区别。

words.memory: 不孤立背：把 arouse 和“arouse the enthusiasm of（调动积极性）”一起记。

words.focus: 重点掌握“奇迹般的；了不起的”，并能在阅读中识别 marvellous。

synonyms.zh: 极好的， 极出色的， 了不起的

example.en: Although the expression “marvellous” may seem familiar, students often misinterpret it when the surrounding paragraph contrasts an immediate result with a consequence that will emerge only later.
example.zh: 尽管“marvellous（奇迹般的）”这一表达看似熟悉，但当上下文把即时结果与之后才会显现的后果作对比时，学生往往会误解它。

questions.prompt: Even though “_____ finance committee” occurs frequently in formal texts, its force becomes fully apparent only when the reader identifies the clause that it modifies and the assumption that the writer expects the audience to accept.
